description: The connection between Cayley graph diameter in SL₂(ℤ/pℤ) and worst-case continued fraction length over ℤ requires a careful lifting argument. The semigroup Γ_{1,...,5} acts on ℤ via continued fraction matrices, but the mod-p diameter only bounds the mod-p representation length — lifting to integer CF length needs either an effective Bourgain-Kontorovich bound or a separate argument about the surjectivity of the reduction map on short words. This is a theoretical gap that cannot be resolved by additional computation alone; it requires a mathematical proof or explicit citation. The finding text should weaken 'translates directly' to 'suggests an analogy with' until a formal argument is provided.
